title: Fix dashboard not refreshing after incidents are added

requirements.summary: Fix the public-facing Dashboard so that it correctly reflects the current state of incidents after they are created or updated via the admin panel by properly invalidating the React Query cache and ensuring fresh data is always displayed
acceptanceCriteria: After creating an incident in the admin panel, the public dashboard reflects the new incident without requiring a manual page reload.
The dashboard does not display a 'no incidents' or empty state when one or more incidents exist in the backend.
The getAllIncidents React Query hook fetches fresh data on mount and invalidates correctly after any create/update/status-change mutation.
Active incidents appear in the active section and resolved incidents appear in the resolved section with correct grouping.

file_operations.description: Update the mutation hooks (useCreateIncident, useAddUpdate, useChangeStatus) to properly invalidate the 'allIncidents' query cache after successful mutations. Ensure that the queryClient.invalidateQueries call uses the correct query key matching the getAllIncidents hook. Add proper error handling and ensure mutations are awaited before invalidation occurs.

Configure the useGetAllIncidents hook to refetch on mount and window focus, ensuring the dashboard always displays the latest data. Add refetchOnMount: true and refetchOnWindowFocus: true to the query options. Verify that the loading and error states are correctly handled and that the 'no incidents' empty state only appears when the incidents array is genuinely empty after a successful fetch.
